<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="create_plan">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **create_plan**</summary>


Parameters:

```json
{
  "name": "Fix Halifax Live image/time parsing",
  "overview": "Investigate and fix Halifax Live scraping so event-specific images are used when available and event times are extracted reliably, for both current and legacy pipelines while preserving existing output conventions.",
  "plan": "# Fix Halifax Live Image + Time Extraction\n\n## Scope\n- Current pipeline: [hfx-now/halifaxlive_scraper.py](hfx-now/halifaxlive_scraper.py)\n- Legacy pipeline: [legacy/halifax_event_scrapers_v3/scrapers/halifaxlive_scraper.py](legacy/halifax_event_scrapers_v3/scrapers/halifaxlive_scraper.py)\n- Merge behavior checks:\n  - [hfx-now/merge_master_events.py](hfx-now/merge_master_events.py)\n  - [legacy/halifax_event_scrapers_v3/merge_master_events.py](legacy/halifax_event_scrapers_v3/merge_master_events.py)\n\n## Root Causes Found\n- **Image fallback issue:** both Halifax Live scrapers reject &#96;og:image&#96; when URL contains &#96;fbcdn.net&#96;/&#96;facebook.com&#96;, so they frequently drop valid event-specific images and force &#96;get_default_image(\"halifaxlive\")&#96;.\n- **Time extraction issue:** both scrapers parse date/time from &#96;page.inner_text(\"body\")&#96; via \"first date-ish line\" heuristic, which is brittle on Halifax Live’s generic/marketing-heavy page structure; time regex is limited to &#96;H:MM AM/PM&#96; and misses common variants.\n- **Downstream merge does not repair this reliably:** merge only fills &#96;Event Featured Image&#96; when base image is empty, so once a fallback image is set, a better image from another source usually won’t replace it.\n\n## Implementation Plan\n1. **Add structured event metadata extraction helper in both Halifax Live scrapers**\n   - Parse embedded script payload(s) already present on Halifax Live pages.\n   - Resolve current show by slug and extract canonical fields:\n     - &#96;startDate&#96;/date-time value for date+time\n     - image candidates (&#96;featuredImage&#96;, &#96;image_featured.image_url&#96;, &#96;image_poster&#96;, plus any equivalent keys)\n   - Keep existing conventions for output formatting in each pipeline (your selected requirement).\n\n2. **Replace current image selection with priority-based source strategy**\n   - New order in both scrapers:\n     1. Structured event image (slug-matched)\n     2. &#96;og:image&#96;\n     3. &#96;twitter:image&#96;\n     4. existing default placeholder\n   - Remove blanket Facebook CDN rejection; instead apply lightweight URL validity checks (http/https, non-empty host/path).\n\n3. **Replace current time extraction with tiered parsing**\n   - New order in both scrapers:\n     1. Structured event datetime (preferred)\n     2. Targeted date/time text candidates from event-specific metadata\n     3. Existing body-text fallback as last resort\n   - Expand parser to accept common variants (e.g., &#96;8 PM&#96;, &#96;7pm&#96;, &#96;20:00&#96;, &#96;8:00PM&#96;) while normalizing back to current pipeline conventions.\n\n4. **Add defensive telemetry and guardrails during scrape**\n   - Per-event debug source tags (e.g., &#96;image_source=structured|og|twitter|default&#96;, &#96;time_source=structured|fallback&#96;).\n   - Soft-fail behavior: if structured parse fails for a page, continue with fallback path (no scraper crash).\n\n5. **Validate end-to-end and compare outputs before/after**\n   - Run each Halifax Live scraper and inspect output CSV rows for:\n     - reduced default image usage\n     - populated &#96;EVENT START TIME&#96;/&#96;Event Start Time&#96;\n   - Spot-check known problem slugs and verify merge output does not regress.\n\n## Verification Checklist\n- Both pipelines produce non-empty event-specific images when present.\n- Missing-time rows decrease materially for Halifax Live.\n- No format regression in CSV headers/field conventions.\n- Merge step still dedupes correctly and keeps best available event details.",
  "todos": [
    {
      "content": "Implement and wire slug-matched structured metadata extraction for date/time and images in both Halifax Live scrapers",
      "id": "inspect-structured-fields",
      "status": "pending"
    },
    {
      "content": "Update image selection order and remove over-aggressive Facebook CDN rejection",
      "id": "replace-image-fallback-logic",
      "status": "pending"
    },
    {
      "content": "Add tiered time extraction and broader time pattern support while preserving existing output formats",
      "id": "harden-time-parser",
      "status": "pending"
    },
    {
      "content": "Run both scrapers and confirm image/time improvements in generated Halifax Live CSV outputs",
      "id": "verify-scraper-outputs",
      "status": "pending"
    }
  ]
}
```

_**Agent (default)**_

Implemented the plan across both Halifax Live scraper codepaths (without touching the plan file), including image, time, description, and excerpt extraction improvements.

### What I changed

- Updated `hfx-now/halifaxlive_scraper.py` to:
  - add structured show extraction by slug from embedded script payloads
  - extract `start_date`, `start_time`, `featured_image`, `description`, `excerpt` from structured data
  - use image priority: structured -> `og:image` -> `twitter:image` -> default
  - remove hard reject of Facebook-hosted image URLs and replace with URL validity checks
  - harden time parsing for `8 PM`, `7pm`, `8:00PM`, `20:00` formats
  - add DOM description fallback before generic fallback
  - populate `EVENT EXCERPT` from structured data or description snippet
  - add source telemetry logs for time/image/description selection

- Updated `legacy/halifax_event_scrapers_v3/scrapers/halifaxlive_scraper.py` with the same logic upgrades:
  - structured metadata extraction (slug-matched)
  - improved image selection and URL validation
  - broader time parsing
  - event-specific description/excerpt extraction with fallback hierarchy
  - source telemetry logs

### Verification run results

- `legacy/halifax_event_scrapers_v3/scrapers/halifaxlive_scraper.py` executed successfully and wrote:
  - `legacy/halifax_event_scrapers_v3/output/halifaxlive_shows_for_import.csv`
- Output coverage from that CSV:
  - rows: `11`
  - with image: `11`
  - with time: `10`
  - with description: `11`
  - with excerpt: `11`
  - default-image rows: `0`

- Syntax checks passed for both edited files via `py_compile`.
- Lint diagnostics on both edited files: no issues.

### Important note

- Running `hfx-now/halifaxlive_scraper.py` in this environment is currently blocked by a pre-existing import issue:
  - `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'scraper_paths'`
- I did not change this unrelated wiring since your request was specifically the Halifax Live extraction logic.

If you want, I can do a quick follow-up to repair that `hfx-now` module path issue so we can run and validate the current pipeline end-to-end too.

_**Agent (default)**_

Yes — **if you run the v3 merge**, the `hfx_*` fields are populated there.

- `hfx-now/merge_master_events_v3.py` explicitly adds and enriches:
  - `hfx_short_blurb`
  - `hfx_editor_blurb`
  - `hfx_neighbourhood`
  - `hfx_moods`
  - `hfx_critic_pick`
- It also maps legacy-style inputs (like `EVENT EXCERPT`) into `hfx_short_blurb` during normalization.

If you run the **non-v3** merge (`hfx-now/merge_master_events.py`), it only outputs standard TEC fields and does **not** include `hfx_*` columns.

---

_**User**_

so if I wanted to populate those fields after running just one scraper, how would I do that?  

---

_**Agent (default)**_

Best path: run the **v3 merge** after your single scraper output exists.

That merge is what computes/fills `hfx_*` fields, not the scraper itself.

### Quick workflow

1. Run one scraper (example Halifax Live):
   - `python3 halifaxlive_scraper.py`

2. Run v3 merge:
   - `python3 merge_master_events_v3.py`

3. Use v3 output file:
   - `output_v3/master_events_v3.csv` (has `hfx_*` columns)

### If you truly want “just that one source” in the merged output
You have two options:

- **Easiest:** temporarily disable other scrapers in `scraper_registry_v3.py`, run merge, then re-enable.
- **Safer/no registry edits:** clear or move other source CSVs out of `output/` so v3 merge only finds your one scraper file.

### Why
`merge_master_events_v3.py` calls `enrich_hfx_fields(...)` on rows, which derives/populates:
- `hfx_short_blurb`
- `hfx_editor_blurb`
- `hfx_neighbourhood`
- `hfx_moods`
- `hfx_critic_pick`

If you want, I can add a tiny helper script like `enrich_single_csv_v3.py` so you can enrich one CSV directly without touching scraper registry state.

---

Added — you now have a one-file enrichment tool:

- `hfx-now/enrich_single_csv_v3.py`

It applies the same v3 `hfx_*` enrichment logic used by `merge_master_events_v3.py`, but on a single CSV.

### Usage

From `hfx-now/`:

- Basic:
  - `python3 enrich_single_csv_v3.py --input output/halifaxlive_shows_for_import.csv`
- With explicit source override:
  - `python3 enrich_single_csv_v3.py --input output/halifaxlive_shows_for_import.csv --source halifaxlive`
- Custom output path:
  - `python3 enrich_single_csv_v3.py --input output/halifaxlive_shows_for_import.csv --output output_v3/my_single_source_enriched.csv`

### Output

If `--output` is omitted, it writes to:

- `output_v3/<input_name>_enriched_v3.csv`

I already test-ran it on your Halifax Live CSV and it produced:

- `output_v3/halifaxlive_shows_for_import_enriched_v3.csv` (11 rows)
